question_text: Two springs with different spring constants (k₁ = 40 N/m and k₂ = 60 N/m) are connected in parallel (side by side). A 5 N force is applied to the combination. Which statement best explains what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: Both springs extend by the same amount; the stiffer spring (k₂) carries more of the load

options.label: B
options.text: The springs extend by different amounts because they have different spring constants

options.label: C
options.text: The less stiff spring (k₁) carries all the load because it extends first

options.label: D
options.text: Each spring carries exactly 2.5 N regardless of their spring constants

explanation: In parallel springs, both extend by the same amount (same displacement). However, because F = kx, the stiffer spring (k₂ = 60 N/m) produces a greater force and carries more of the 5 N load. The force distribution is proportional to the spring constants: k₁ carries 40/(40+60) × 5 = 2 N, and k₂ carries 60/(40+60) × 5 = 3 N.
